libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or external service appears necessary. Prefer in-process loopback HTTP tests using JDK HttpServer or WebClient test doubles; no environment variables or system properties are required by the library.

deterministic_setup.reason: The artifact's auto-configuration classes use Spring Boot auto-configuration annotations and conditions, but this dependency is optional and is not present in the normal target-only compile classpath.
The module has a runtime implementation dependency on Boot HTTP codecs; tests that exercise WebClientCodecCustomizer need CodecCustomizer on the test compile classpath.
Spring Boot's own tests for this module use ApplicationContextRunner to exercise auto-configuration behavior, which is the normal focused way to test this kind of module.
The WebClient observation auto-configuration is conditional on Boot Micrometer observation properties from this optional module.

risks: Several Spring Boot 4 module dependencies are optional or runtime-only, so tests that only depend on spring-boot-webclient can silently avoid the primary auto-configuration paths.
The requested version is an RC; local Gradle dependency resolution and native-image verification remain authoritative if module metadata differs from the published POM.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Spring Boot WebClient is mainly an auto-configuration/support module; meaningful tests should compile and run against its optional/test support pieces rather than only the target artifact.
